project.name: SylphxAI Cat
project.summary: TypeScript/Bun monorepo for the @sylphx/cat logging and observability package ecosystem.
goals: Provide the modular @sylphx/cat logger core and companion observability packages.
Own package-level logging, formatting, transport, serialization, redaction, tracing, OTLP export, and tail-sampling behavior.
Maintain package docs, examples, benchmarks, tests, and the Cat documentation site.
nonGoals: Own application-specific log schemas, retention policy, alert policy, or telemetry backend operations.
Own OpenTelemetry collectors, external vendors, or product runtime observability dashboards.
Own enterprise engineering doctrine, standards, or project manifest schema.

owns.name: cat-package-ecosystem
owns.description: The core logger package plus companion packages for pretty output, file/http transports, OTLP, tracing, redaction, and tail sampling.

owns.name: cat-docs-examples-benchmarks
owns.description: Repository docs, examples, benchmarks, API references, and package-level tests for the Cat ecosystem.

doesNotOwn: Application-specific logging policy, telemetry backend configuration, secrets, retention, alerts, or dashboards.
OpenTelemetry collector infrastructure, external observability vendors, or consuming service deployment policy.
Enterprise doctrine and manifest schema owned by SylphxAI/doctrine.

allowedDependencies.surface: project manifest schema and engineering doctrine

forbiddenCouplings: Do not encode a consuming application's log taxonomy, alerting policy, or telemetry vendor configuration into Cat core packages.
Do not make Cat depend on product-specific runtime services or dashboards.
Do not fork doctrine standards into this repository.

delivery.deployPath: Pull requests and merge queue run Bun/Turborepo CI; main-branch Release delegates to SylphxAI/.github reusable release workflow for package publication.
delivery.productionProof: Passing bun run check, tests, build, relevant benchmarks for behavior changes, successful release workflow, and package-registry/readme readback for published versions.

gaps.description: CI exists for pull requests and merge queue, but required branch protection contexts are not recorded in this manifest baseline.
gaps.target: central ADR-29 required-context rollout or repo-local branch protection audit

gaps.description: README includes performance and bundle-size claims; keep benchmark and generated-doc freshness gated before treating the claims as production proof.
gaps.target: next package performance or docs release

gaps.description: Package publication is forward-only once published; add a runbook for failed publishes, bad package versions, and registry recovery.
